ucs.title: [LLM] Axiom / Group 72 accessibility-features backdoor (Sticky Keys / Utilman swap + IFEO debugger)
ucs.description: Hunts Axiom's signature long-dwell persistence: replacing or hijacking Windows accessibility binaries (sethc.exe, utilman.exe, osk.exe) so that hitting Shift x5 or Win+U at the RDP/console login screen yields a SYSTEM cmd.exe. This backdoor – often paired with Hikit – is specifically called out in Novetta's 'Operation SMN' Axiom report and is a hallmark of this cluster.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem WHERE (Filesystem.file_name IN ("sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe","magnify.exe","narrator.exe","displayswitch.exe","atbroker.exe") AND Filesystem.action IN ("created","modified","renamed","overwritten") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\System32\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\SysWOW64\\*") AND NOT Filesystem.process_name IN ("TrustedInstaller.exe","TiWorker.exe","msiexec.exe","wuauclt.exe","poqexec.exe")) BY host Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.user | rename Filesystem.* AS * | eval signal="acc_bin_replaced" | append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Registry WHERE (Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\sethc.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\utilman.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\osk.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\magnify.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\narrator.exe*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Image File Execution Options\\displayswitch.exe*") AND Registry.registry_value_name="Debugger" BY host Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_data Registry.process_name Registry.user | rename Registry.* AS * | eval signal="ifeo_debugger_set" ] | append [ | tstats summariesonly=true count FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes WHERE Processes.parent_process_name="winlogon.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe") BY host Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user | rename Processes.* AS * | eval signal="shell_from_winlogon" ] | stats values(signal) as signals values(file_name) as file values(registry_path) as reg values(process) as cmdline values(user) as user count by host | where mvcount(signals) >= 1 | sort - count
ucs.kql:
let accessibility_bins = dynamic(["sethc.exe","utilman.exe","osk.exe","magnify.exe","narrator.exe","displayswitch.exe","atbroker.exe"]);
let file_swap = DeviceFileEvents
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where FileName in~ (accessibility_bins)
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\System32\", @"\SysWOW64\")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("TrustedInstaller.exe","TiWorker.exe","msiexec.exe","wuauclt.exe","poqexec.exe")
| extend Signal="acc_bin_replaced"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, Signal, FileName, FolderPath, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName, SHA256;
let ifeo_debug = DeviceRegistryEvents
| where RegistryKey has @"\Image File Execution Options\"
| where RegistryValueName =~ "Debugger"
| extend target = tolower(tostring(split(RegistryKey, @"\")[-1]))
| where target in~ (accessibility_bins)
| extend Signal="ifeo_debugger_set"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, Signal, RegistryKey, RegistryValueData, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName;
let winlogon_shell = DeviceProcessEvents
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "winlogon.exe"
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","mshta.exe")
| extend Signal="shell_from_winlogon"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, Signal, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, AccountName, AccountSid;
union file_swap, ifeo_debug, winlogon_shell
| summarize SignalCount=dcount(Signal), Signals=make_set(Signal), Evidence=make_list(pack_all(),20), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceName
| where SignalCount >= 1
| order by SignalCount desc, LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Sticky Keys / Utilman swapping is one of the most-cited Axiom tradecraft items (G0001 T1546.008) and is documented in the Novetta SMN coalition takedown of Hikit. Pairing the file-system replacement, IFEO Debugger registry variant, and the runtime tell (cmd.exe/powershell.exe spawned by winlogon.exe at the login screen) catches all three implementation styles Axiom has rotated through – something

ucs.title: [LLM] Axiom / Group 72 RDP session hijacking via tscon under SYSTEM
ucs.description: Hunts Axiom's documented lateral-movement pattern of using stolen valid accounts to land via RDP, then elevating to SYSTEM and abusing tscon.exe (or a service wrapping it) to take over other users' disconnected RDP sessions without ever supplying their password – classic G0001 T1563.002 tradecraft against aerospace/defense Windows estates.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes WHERE (Processes.process_name="tscon.exe" OR Processes.process="*tscon*\/dest:*" OR (Processes.process_name IN ("sc.exe","net.exe") AND Processes.process="*tscon*")) BY host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_id | rename Processes.* AS * | eval system_ctx=if(match(lower(user),"^(nt authority\\\\system|system|local system)$") OR parent_process_name="services.exe",1,0) | eval has_dest=if(match(process,"(?i)\/dest:"),1,0) | eval is_svc_wrap=if(process_name IN ("sc.exe","net.exe"),1,0) | where system_ctx=1 OR has_dest=1 OR is_svc_wrap=1 | join type=outer host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count as rdp_logons FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE Authentication.authentication_method="RemoteInteractive" OR Authentication.signature_id IN ("4624","4778") BY Authentication.dest Authentication.user | rename Authentication.dest AS host Authentication.user AS rdp_user ] | stats values(process) as cmds values(user) as actor values(parent_process_name) as parents values(rdp_user) as rdp_users sum(system_ctx) as system_hits sum(has_dest) as dest_hits sum(is_svc_wrap) as svc_hits by host firstTime lastTime | where (system_hits>0 AND dest_hits>0) OR svc_hits>0 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
let tscon_exec = DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName =~ "tscon.exe" or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\btscon(\.exe)?\b"
| extend SystemCtx = (InitiatingProcessAccountName in~ ("system","local system") or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "services.exe")
| extend HasDest = ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/dest:"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, DeviceId, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName, SystemCtx, HasDest, AccountName;
let svc_wrap = DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName in~ ("sc.exe","net.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)tscon" and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(create|config|binpath|new-service)"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, DeviceId, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessAccountName, AccountName
| extend SvcWrap = true;
let rdp_landing = DeviceLogonEvents
| where LogonType in ("RemoteInteractive","Network") and ActionType =~ "LogonSuccess"
| summarize RdpUsers=make_set(AccountName,25), RdpLogonCount=count() by DeviceId, bin(Timestamp, 1d);
union tscon_exec, svc_wrap
| join kind=leftouter rdp_landing on DeviceId
| where SystemCtx == true or HasDest == true or SvcWrap == true
| summarize Hits=count(), Cmds=make_set(ProcessCommandLine,20), Actors=make_set(InitiatingProcessAccountName,10), RdpUsers=any(RdpUsers), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp) by DeviceName, DeviceId
| where Hits >= 1
| order by LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Axiom (G0001) is one of the few state-aligned crews where MITRE explicitly lists T1563.002 RDP Hijacking alongside T1021.001 RDP and T1078 Valid Accounts – the exact chain you'd expect from a long-dwell PRC espionage operator pivoting between jump-hosts in aerospace/defense networks. tscon.exe legitimately almost never runs under SYSTEM, and never legitimately takes /dest: to a session it doesn't
